title: mystacinus lanceolatus
intro: Mystacinus lanceolatus, also known as the lance-leaved forget-me-not, is a type of flowering plant native to New Zealand. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and its potential anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Mystacinus lanceolatus include its use as a treatment for fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in the form of a tea or infusion, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water.
